name: Homework 0: Environment Setup
description: Install the JDK and Gradle, then submit a screenshot of java -version.

name: Lab 1: Java Refresher
description: Implement the warm-up classes and pass the provided JUnit tests.

name: Homework 1: Inheritance & Polymorphism
description: Model a shape hierarchy and demonstrate dynamic dispatch.

name: Design Exercise: UML Diagrams
description: Submit a class diagram and a short design rationale.

name: Homework 2: Generics & Collections
description: Build a generic container and benchmark it against the JDK collections.

name: Lab 2: Design Patterns
description: Refactor the starter code to apply the Strategy and Observer patterns.

name: Project Milestone 1: Proposal
description: Submit a one-page proposal for your term project.

name: Homework 3: Concurrency
description: Implement a thread-safe bounded buffer and discuss its invariants.

name: Problem Set 1: Vectors
description: Vector operations and geometry in R^3.

name: Problem Set 2: Dot & Cross Products
description: Projections, areas, and volumes.

name: WebAssign HW 3: Lines & Planes
description: Complete the online problem set on WebAssign.

name: Problem Set 4: Partial Derivatives
description: Gradients, tangent planes, and linearization.

name: Problem Set 5: Multiple Integrals
description: Double and triple integrals in various coordinate systems.

name: WebAssign HW 6: Gradient & Directional Derivatives
description: Complete the online problem set on WebAssign.

name: Problem Set 7: Vector Fields
description: Line integrals and conservative fields.

name: Problem Set 8: Green's Theorem
description: Applications of Green's Theorem.

name: Reading Response 1
description: One paragraph reflecting on Chapter 1.

name: Discussion: Nature vs Nurture
description: Post your view and reply to two classmates.

name: Reading Response 2
description: One paragraph reflecting on Chapter 3.

name: Research Methods Worksheet
description: Identify variables and designs in the given studies.

name: Discussion: Memory
description: Discuss a real-world example of a memory bias.

name: Reading Response 3
description: One paragraph reflecting on Chapter 6.

name: Article Critique
description: Critique a peer-reviewed psychology article (2 pages).

name: Reading Response 4
description: One paragraph reflecting on Chapter 9.

name: Diagnostic Essay
description: Ungraded writing sample to assess your baseline.

name: Rhetorical Analysis Draft
description: First draft of your rhetorical analysis (3-4 pages).

name: Peer Review 1
description: Provide written feedback on two peers' drafts.

name: Rhetorical Analysis Final
description: Revised, polished rhetorical analysis.

name: Annotated Bibliography
description: Ten annotated sources for the argument essay.

name: Argument Essay Draft
description: First draft of your argument essay (5-6 pages).

name: Peer Review 2
description: Provide written feedback on two peers' argument drafts.

name: Argument Essay Final
description: Revised, polished argument essay.

name: Map Quiz Prep Worksheet
description: Label the major regions discussed in week 1.

name: Primary Source Analysis 1
description: Analyze a 16th-century primary source (2 pages).

name: Discussion: Columbian Exchange
description: Discuss the global impact of the Columbian Exchange.

name: Primary Source Analysis 2
description: Analyze an Enlightenment-era primary source (2 pages).

name: Reading Quiz: Enlightenment
description: Short quiz on the assigned Enlightenment readings.

name: Document-Based Question Essay
description: Write a DBQ essay using the provided documents.

name: Discussion: Industrial Revolution
description: Discuss the social consequences of industrialization.

name: Primary Source Analysis 3
description: Analyze a 19th-century primary source (2 pages).
